bead.title: Investigation/report beads must write evidence to .ddx/executions/, not /tmp
bead.description: B15a (ddx-8d51b137) wrote its investigation report to /tmp/feat-011-status.md. /tmp is outside the repo, so the post-merge review couldn't see the evidence and flagged BLOCK. Manual workaround: commit 7f943dc1 copied the report into .ddx/executions/20260502T020500-79017d32/. Pattern fix: any bead whose AC says 'output a report' or 'document findings' should write under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ (or another in-repo path) so the evidence survives reboots and is reviewable. Either: (a) update the execute-bead system prompt to instruct agents to use .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ for investigation outputs, or (b) extend the AC template guidance in CLAUDE.md / SKILL.md to require in-repo evidence paths, or (c) both.
bead.acceptance: 1. execute-bead system prompt (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText/AgentText) instructs agents to write investigation/report outputs under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ rather than /tmp. 2. CLAUDE.md or relevant guidance file documents the convention. 3. A test or demonstration bead with 'output a report at <path>' AC writes to .ddx/executions/ when executed.
